feedback: Honestly the transcription is magic. Joined my first call and had a clean summary in my inbox before I even left the meeting.
Setup was way more painful than I expected. It asked for calendar permissions, then Zoom permissions, then a separate bot install. Gave up halfway and came back the next day.
I love that the first summary showed up automatically. Didn't have to configure anything to see value.
Couldn't figure out how to get the bot to actually join my meeting. The docs assume you already know what an 'integration' is.
Pricing is sneaky. Got 200 minutes free, hit the wall mid-month with zero warning, then found out the real plan is $30/mo. Felt baited.
Switched back to Otter. The onboarding there just worked and I didn't have to grant five different permissions.
The auto summaries are genuinely good, better than the competitor I tried last year.
Spent 20 minutes trying to share a meeting workspace with my teammate and never figured it out. No clear 'invite' button anywhere.
First-run experience is confusing — I landed on an empty dashboard with no sample meeting or 'do this first' guide.
Calendar permission screen scared me off. It asked for full read/write access and didn't explain why. I almost didn't continue.
Once it was set up it was great, but getting there took three tries across two days.
I gave up. Too much setup for something that's supposed to save me time.
The demo on their site sold me, but the actual product made me do all the setup myself before I could see anything like the demo.
Connecting Slack was smooth and the first shared note landed in our channel automatically — nice touch.
Not worth it. Free tier limits hit immediately and the upgrade prompt appeared right as I was getting value, killed the trust.
Wish there had been a guided checklist. I had to guess the order: connect calendar, install bot, set recording defaults, invite team.
Loved the instant value but hated that pricing limits weren't shown until after I'd already invested time setting everything up.
